title: Fever Without Source - Pediatric UTI
chief_complaint: 18-month-old girl with fever 39.5°C for 2 days, irritability, decreased appetite, and foul-smelling urine. No cough, no diarrhea.

hidden_data.ua: Leukocyte esterase: Positive, Nitrites: Positive, WBC: 25/HPF, Bacteria: Many

correct_diagnosis: Febrile Urinary Tract Infection (Pyelonephritis vs Cystitis)
key_learning_points: UTI = most common cause of fever without source in young children
Renal US after first febrile UTI
Consider VCUG if abnormal US or recurrent

patient_responses.fever: She's been burning up for 2 days
patient_responses.urine: Her diapers smell really strong and foul
patient_responses.drinking: She's not drinking as much as usual

hints.imaging: Renal ultrasound after first febrile UTI

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Febrile UTI in a young child.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a UTI. The positive UA + culture confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Febrile UTI: Treat with cephalexin or cefixime for 7-14 days. Renal US after first febrile UTI to rule out anatomical abnormalities. VCUG if abnormal US or recurrent UTIs. Encourage hydration and frequent voiding.
